title: Williams (Respondent) v Central Bank of Nigeria (Appellant)

metadata.title: Williams (Respondent) v Central Bank of Nigeria (Appellant)
metadata.summary: (3) Subject to the preceding provisions of this section, an action by a beneficiary to recover trust property or in respect of any breach of trust, not being an action for which a period of limitation is prescribed by any other provision of this Act, shall not be brought after the expiration of s...
extracts: (3) Subject to the preceding provisions of this section, an action by a beneficiary to recover trust property or in respect of any breach of trust, not being an action for which a period of limitation is prescribed by any other provision of this Act, shall not be brought after the expiration of six years from the date on which the right of action accrued.”

Section 38(1) of the Limitation Act 1980, defines the terms “trust” and “trustee” as having “the same meanings, respectively, as in the Trustee Act 1925”. This is a reference to section 68(17) of the Trustee Act 1925, which provides that subject to immaterial exceptions,
metadata.title_key: williams v central bank of nigeria
